summary: Icon-only close button wrapped only a 20px icon with no padding (~20x20 target)
recommendation: Add padding (p-1.5) to reach a ~44px target

summary: Accessible name depends on the optional dialogId; without it aria-labelledby resolves to null and the dialog is unnamed
recommendation: Require dialogId or add an aria-label fallback; documented in Accessibility Notes

summary: Dialog body copy was not programmatically associated as the dialog description
recommendation: Add dialogDescriptionId and wire aria-describedby to the body text

summary: Dialog title used h3 in the demo but h2 in docs, and is teleported out of the heading outline
recommendation: Standardise on h2

summary: x-trap sets initial focus to the first focusable control (close X); for a destructive confirm dialog initial focus on the least destructive action is safer
recommendation: Consider setting initial focus to Cancel for destructive confirmations

summary: Multi-select mode does not set aria-multiselectable on the listbox even though acceptMultiple is supported and aria-selected is set per option
recommendation: Bind aria-multiselectable=true when acceptMultiple is enabled and document it

summary: Option buttons lack tabindex=-1 so they can enter the tab sequence; the activedescendant pattern should keep real focus on the input
recommendation: Add tabindex=-1 to option buttons (matches CommandPalette)

summary: Quick Start option buttons lack tabindex=-1
recommendation: Add tabindex=-1 to the option buttons in the snippet

summary: Listbox popup has no accessible name
recommendation: Add an aria-label to the role=listbox element

summary: Menu does not implement type-ahead (typing a character to move focus to a matching item), a WAI-ARIA menu recommendation
recommendation: Add first-character type-ahead focus movement

summary: Nested menu items are flattened into one role=menu list with visual indentation; the parent/child relationship is conveyed to screen readers by aria-label only
recommendation: Documented mitigation exists; for deep menus consider a true nested role=menu with aria-haspopup

summary: Composed dialog title uses sr-only h3 while other dialogs standardise on h2 and it is teleported out of the outline
recommendation: Use h2 for the dialog title

summary: Filtered result count is not announced to screen readers via a live region
recommendation: Add an aria-live=polite status announcing the number of matches

summary: Six inline-editor trigger buttons all expose the same accessible name Edit; the field name is a nearby unassociated paragraph so screen reader users cannot tell them apart
recommendation: Give each Edit button a distinguishing accessible name (aria-label like Edit display name, or aria-labelledby referencing the field label)

summary: Committing an edit updates the displayed value with no polite announcement
recommendation: Add an aria-live=polite region announcing the saved value or a Saved status

summary: While editing, Tab is trapped in the editor and the only exits are Enter (commit) or Escape (revert); a user cannot Tab out without committing or reverting
recommendation: Keep the Enter/Escape exit method communicated near the field (the demo does via help text); document as a required affordance

summary: Copy failures (insecure context or blocked clipboard) are logged to console only; users get no visible or announced feedback
recommendation: Surface a failure state (visible text and aria-live) when copyToClipboard cannot write

summary: The coupled and decoupled tablists on the demo page share the same aria-label Account settings, making the two tab groups indistinguishable to screen reader users
recommendation: Give each tablist a distinct aria-label

summary: Arrow-key navigation uses automatic activation (focus and selection move together); for panels with expensive content manual activation is recommended
recommendation: Offer or document manual activation (move focus on arrows, activate on Enter/Space) for heavy panels

summary: Step panels use aria-live=polite but are toggled with x-show (visibility only); changing visibility does not reliably trigger live-region announcements of the new step
recommendation: Announce the active step via a single dedicated visually-hidden aria-live region updated on step change

summary: role=slider binds aria-valuemax to maxWidth which defaults to null, so the required aria-valuemax property is absent whenever maxWidth is unset (the demo and Quick Start both leave it unset)
recommendation: Derive a numeric max (last breakpoint or parent width) for aria-valuemax, or require maxWidth

summary: Active breakpoint jump button is indicated by background colour only with no programmatic selected state
recommendation: Add aria-pressed (or aria-current) to the active breakpoint button

summary: The scrollable content panel (x-ref tocContent) is not keyboard focusable and has no role or name, so keyboard-only users cannot scroll it directly and can only jump via TOC buttons
recommendation: Add tabindex=0 and role=region with an accessible name to the scroll container

summary: The scrollable feed has no focusable items and no tabindex, so keyboard-only users cannot scroll it to trigger load-more, and the demo provides no Load more button
recommendation: Make the container keyboard focusable (tabindex=0) or add a visible Load more button (the docs recommend the button but the demo omits it)

summary: The end-of-content message All posts loaded is not in an aria-live region so completion is not announced
recommendation: Add aria-live=polite to the completion message

summary: Fetch failures (markFailed) reset loading with no user-facing or announced error
recommendation: Show an announced error state with a retry affordance on failure
